title: Navigating the Filesystem
content:
### pwd — Print Working Directory

Shows your current location in the filesystem.

```bash
pwd
# /home/user/projects
```

### ls — List Directory Contents

| Option | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| `-l` | Long format (permissions, size, date) |
| `-a` | Show hidden files (starting with `.`) |
| `-h` | Human-readable sizes (KB, MB, GB) |
| `-R` | Recursive listing |
| `-t` | Sort by modification time |
| `-S` | Sort by file size |

### cd — Change Directory

| Expression | Meaning |
|------------|---------|
| `cd /etc` | Go to absolute path |
| `cd ..` | Go up one directory |
| `cd -` | Go to previous directory |
| `cd ~` or `cd` | Go to home directory |

### pushd / popd — Directory Stack

```bash
pushd /var/log   # switch and push to stack
pushd /tmp       # switch again
popd             # go back to /var/log
popd             # go back to original
```

### tree — Visual Directory Tree

```bash
tree -L 2 /etc   # show 2 levels deep
```

### realpath — Resolve Symlinks

```bash
realpath ~/..
# /home
```
codeExample:
pwd
ls -lah /home
cd /tmp
pushd /var/log
pwd
popd
tree -L 1 /etc

quiz.question: What does `cd -` do?
options: Takes you to the previous directory
Takes you to the root directory
Takes you to the home directory

quiz.question: Which ls option shows hidden files?
